title: Principal Project Engineer — Offshore Oil & Gas
description_raw:
Spencer Ogden are seeking a Principal Project Engineer for an upstream oil and gas client operating in the Central North Sea. This role is engaged outside IR35.

The Principal Project Engineer will provide senior technical leadership for a subsea tieback project, managing the mechanical and subsea engineering disciplines from FEED through to hook-up and commissioning.

Key responsibilities:
- Act as lead technical authority for mechanical and subsea engineering across the project
- Manage third-party engineering contractors delivering detailed design
- Chair technical review meetings (HAZOP, 3D model reviews, constructability reviews)
- Issue and approve engineering change requests within the project MoC process
- Coordinate with the offshore operations team on brownfield interface management
- Report project status and technical risks to the project director

Profile:
- Degree in mechanical engineering; Chartered Engineer (IMechE)
- 15+ years in offshore oil and gas mechanical or subsea engineering
- Previous principal or lead engineer experience on FEED or EPC projects
- BOSIET/HUET; offshore medicals current

Rate: £700–£750/day outside IR35
Duration: 24 months
Start: January 2027
Location: Aberdeen + offshore trips
